request.question: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

hypothesis.intervention: replace sucrose with trehalose as the cryoprotectant in the freezing medium
hypothesis.threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than the standard DMSO protocol
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All six core fields are explicitly stated in the hypothesis with a concrete intervention, named cell line, defined comparator, named outcome, numeric threshold, and a biological mechanism.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: trehalose DMSO cryopreservation HeLa viability trypan blue
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

references.title: Trehalose-based cryopreservation of mammalian cells
references.relevance_note: Covers trehalose as a cryoprotectant in mammalian systems.

literature_qc.explanation: Papers using trehalose as a cryoprotectant exist in the literature, primarily in the context of cell preservation and lyophilization. No paper was found combining trehalose with HeLa cells specifically using trypan blue viability as the primary endpoint.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

risks.description: Control strategy may miss process-specific artifacts in handling or reagent preparation.
risks.mitigation: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

plan.objective: To determine the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved with trehalose compared to DMSO.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be divided into two groups: one group will be cryopreserved using trehalose as the cryoprotectant, while the other will use the standard DMSO protocol. Each condition will be replicated three times to ensure statistical validity. Post-thaw viability will be assessed using trypan blue exclusion after a 30-minute recovery period at 37°C.
controls: HeLa cells cryopreserved with 10% DMSO in freezing medium.
HeLa cells without cryopreservation, assessed for viability immediately after thawing.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 48 h | Cell Culture
Culture HeLa cells in DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin at 37°C, 5% CO2 until they reach 80% confluency (approximately 48 h).
Equipment: Thermo Scientific Heracell 150i incubator
Reagents: DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092), Fetal Bovine Serum (FBS, Sigma, F7524), Penicillin-Streptomycin (Gibco, 15140-122)
Expected outcome: Cells reach 80% confluency.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Cryoprotectant Preparation
Prepare 10% trehalose solution in DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092) by dissolving 10 g of trehalose dihydrate (Sigma, T9531) in 100 mL of DMEM at room temperature. Filter sterilize using a 0.22 µm filter.
Equipment: Sterile 50 mL conical tubes, 0.22 µm filter unit
Reagents: Trehalose dihydrate (Sigma, T9531), DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092)
Expected outcome: Sterile 10% trehalose solution prepared.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Cryoprotectant Preparation
Prepare 10% DMSO solution in DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092) by mixing 10 mL of DMSO (Sigma, D8418) with 90 mL of DMEM at room temperature. Filter sterilize using a 0.22 µm filter.
Equipment: Sterile 50 mL conical tubes, 0.22 µm filter unit
Reagents: DMSO (Sigma, D8418), DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092)
Expected outcome: Sterile 10% DMSO solution prepared.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Cell Harvesting
Detach HeLa cells using 0.25% trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200-056) for 5 min at 37°C. Neutralize with 10% FBS in DMEM, centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature, and resuspend cells in DMEM.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, Thermo Scientific Heracell 150i incubator
Reagents: 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200-056), DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092), FBS (Sigma, F7524)
Expected outcome: Cells detached and resuspended in DMEM.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Cryopreservation
Aliquot 1 mL of cell suspension (1 x 10^6 cells/mL) into two cryovials. To one vial, add 100 µL of 10% trehalose solution and to the other, add 100 µL of 10% DMSO solution. Mix gently by inverting the vials.
Equipment: Cryovials (Nunc, 377310)
Reagents: 10% trehalose solution, 10% DMSO solution
Expected outcome: Cells mixed with cryoprotectants in cryovials.

DAY 1 | 24 h | Cryopreservation
Place the cryovials in a Mr. Frosty freezing container (Thermo Fisher) at -80°C for 24 h to allow slow freezing.
Equipment: Mr. Frosty freezing container
Expected outcome: Cryovials cooled to -80°C.

DAY 2 | 10 min | Thawing
Remove cryovials from -80°C and quickly thaw in a 37°C water bath for approximately 2-3 min until just thawed. Immediately transfer to a 15 mL conical tube containing 9 mL of DMEM with 10% FBS to dilute the cryoprotectant.
Equipment: 37°C water bath, 15 mL conical tubes
Expected outcome: Cells thawed and diluted in DMEM.

DAY 2 | 40 min | Cell Recovery
Centrifuge the cells at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature. Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL of DMEM with 10% FBS. Incubate at 37°C for 30 min to allow recovery.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge
Expected outcome: Cells resuspended and allowed to recover.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Viability Assessment
Add 100 µL of trypan blue solution (0.4% w/v, Sigma, T8154) to 100 µL of cell suspension. Mix gently and load 10 µL onto a hemocytometer. Count viable (unstained) and non-viable (stained) cells under a microscope (Olympus CKX53) at 10x magnification.
Equipment: Hemocytometer (Hausser Scientific), Olympus CKX53 microscope
Reagents: Trypan blue solution (0.4% w/v, Sigma, T8154)
Expected outcome: Viability percentage calculated.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Calculate the percentage of viable cells for each condition (trehalose and DMSO) using the formula: (number of viable cells / total cells) × 100. Compare the post-thaw viability between the two groups using a two-tailed t-test with a significance level of p < 0.05.
Expected outcome: Statistical comparison of post-thaw viability between trehalose and DMSO groups.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Trehalose provides better membrane stabilization than DMSO during cryopreservation.
HeLa cells can be effectively cryopreserved using trehalose without compromising viability.
The trypan blue exclusion method accurately reflects cell viability.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability with trehalose is at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO.
Proceed if viability results are consistent across all three replicates.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same passage number of HeLa cells for all experiments (passage 20-25).
Ensure all reagents are from the same batch to minimize variability.
Calibrate the hemocytometer and microscope before counting cells.
feedback_incorporated:
PRIOR SCIENTIST CORRECTIONS FOR SIMILAR EXPERIMENTS:
A scientist has previously flagged these specific issues on similar comparative_cryopreservation experiments. You MUST implement each correction below in your output unless it directly contradicts the current hypothesis:
1. replicate gate in plan text
2. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting
3. change one

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than the standard DMSO protocol improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved with trehalose compared to DMSO.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
